Facilities - Central
Central Public Health Center
Address:
Central Public
 Health Center at Southeastern Live Well Center
5101
 Market St, San Diego, CA 92114 STE 2000
Phone:
619-229-5400
Hours:
Open:
 Monday –  Wednesday, & Friday 8am to 5pm
Thursday 10am-5pm*
Closed:
 Saturday, Sunday and Holidays
*Clinic hours may be extended to meet community needs.
About:
The Central Public Health Center (PHC)
 provides access to preventative health services including public
 health nurse visitation programs. Below are the services offered:
Immunizations: Provides a daily immunization clinic for:
Children and adults who do not have health insurance;
Children and adults whose health insurance does not cover
 vaccinations
Medi-Cal participants ages 0-18
Alaskan Native or American Indian ages 0-18
Persons 6 month and older in need of influenza (flu) vaccine
 regardless of health coverage.
We do not provide
 travel related immunizations.
For an appointment,
 please call 619-229-5400 or for self-service please schedule
 an
on-line appointment
.
Tuberculosis (TB) Testing and Treatment: Offers Tuberculosis
 testing and treatment to assist in the detection, control and to
 prevent the spread of TB through treatment and case management.
Monday and Wednesday Only
LTBI Treatment on
 Thursdays, with appointment only availabilty
Sexually Transmitted Disease (STD): Offers regular testing and
 treatment for most STDs, vaccines for HPV, hepatitis A and B and HIV
 testing. All services are offered on the basis of risk assessments,
 physical examinations, and/or laboratory test results.
Tuesday, Wednesday and Friday; 9:00am-4:00pm
Nurse Home Visitation Programs: Provides nurse home visitation
 services to low income, first-time mothers prior to their 28th week
 of pregnancy; as well as; services to high risk infants, pregnant or
 postpartum women and their children. The nurses provide health
 assessments, health education, parenting education and referrals to
 community services.
Services:
Immunizations
TB
 Testing
HIV
 Screening
STD
 Testing
Naloxone
 (sandiegocounty.gov)
Provides access to preventive health services &
 information (nurse home visits with emphasis on communicable disease
 control and maternal child health, particularly for high-risk
 families and individuals)
Payment Methods:
The Public Health Center is NOW accepting Visa, Mastercard,
 American Express, and Discover as payment methods. The cards may be
 swiped, inserted, or tapped (NFC). Google and Apple Pay are also
 accepted as a method of payment using the NFC tap function.
No fees will be charged to the customer for using their
 credit/debit card.
